metadata.title: There Is No Nothing: A Premise-Free Operational Foundation and an Open Verification Platform for Smithian Fold Theory
metadata.description:
This inaugural methods and foundation paper introduces the third clean-room reconstruction of Smithian Fold Theory and the Ernos Labs Open Source Science Platform and Knowledge Tree. It publishes the complete machine-readable and human-readable derivation traces for the premise-free operational theorem there is no nothing and the forward-forced structural One.

The accompanying standard-library-first Python repository implements one fail-closed admission engine for registration, dependency and provenance closure, zero-parameter and no-axiom enforcement, generated candidate enumeration, exactly-one-survivor forcing, minimality, named-shape uniqueness, adverse controls, cryptographic sealing, implementation-distinct recomputation, empirical target custody and publication gates. The release records 60 unit, adverse-control and end-to-end tests, 1,263 of 1,263 core-engine executable lines observed, 15 engine modules and a fresh ordered rerun of both admitted derivations.

The paper gives full candidate, decision, proof, control, source, validator, seal and receipt identities; an engine threat model; the blind empirical protocol; the open licensing and Ernos Labs conformance model; a supersession ledger for prior SFT generations; and a file-level paper-to-evidence map. It explicitly does not claim that the foundation branch or Theory of Everything is complete. Later papers are issued only when a frozen scientific branch closes through the engine, followed eventually by the complete TOE rewrite.
metadata.notes: Copyright © 2026 Maria Smith. Paper and documentation: CC BY 4.0. Repository code: Apache-2.0. Ernos Labs is a separate scientific-standards conformance designation. This is an inaugural methods release containing two admitted formal claims; it is not a completed foundation-branch or TOE paper.
